ip_outcome_note: Confirmed Sigilyph: geometric totem-like flying Pokémon with single large eye and teal/yellow protrusions matches swsh9-66 record and Bulbapedia. National Pokedex no. 561. Reverse holofoil variant.

trivia_bullets.text: Sigilyph's design is confirmed by Bulbapedia as inspired primarily by the Nazca Lines (Peruvian desert geoglyphs, ~500 BCE–500 CE), particularly the hummingbird figure. Secondary influence: kachina dolls from Hopi/Pueblo traditions, accounting for the colorful geometric face and pendant limbs. Name combines sigil + glyph, with possible sylph (air spirit) reference.

"Psychic power allows these Pokémon to fly. Some say they were the guardians of an ancient city. Others say they were the guardians' emissaries." Deliberately ambiguous — guardian vs. emissary. In-game Sigilyph are found at Relic Castle in Unova, the ruins of an ancient city.

Sigilyph is Unova-native (Gen V, National Pokedex no. 561). The corpus holds Golett (another ancient-construct Pokémon from Relic Castle / Desert Resort area of Unova), creating a nascent ancient-civilization cluster. Wave 17 vision pass flagged this adjacency.

This copy is the reverse holofoil variant of BRS-066. Brilliant Stars (SWSH9) released February 25, 2022; headlined by Arceus VSTAR. Sigilyph appears as an uncommon; reverse holo is the low-cost collector variant of an already-low-rarity card.

related_cards.name: Cofagrigus
related_cards.note: Egyptian-inspired Unova Pokémon, same lore cluster
